index.name: Nifty Energy Index
index.description: Nifty Energy tracks 10 companies spanning entire energy value chain: upstream (ONGC exploration, Coal India mining), midstream (NTPC generation, Power Grid transmission, GAIL pipelines), downstream (Reliance O2C refining, IOC marketing), and new energy (Adani Green solar). India's energy demand growing 5% annually — fastest globally — underpinned by 1.4B population, rising per-capita consumption, and electrification. Structural tailwind: renewable energy transition targets 50% capacity by 2032 (currently 35%), requiring ₹10 Lakh Cr capex. Coal India monopoly ensures stable cash flows (dividend yield 6-8%) but faces long-term coal-demand decline. NTPC/Power Grid benefit from transmission upgrade capex (₹1 Lakh Cr+ CapEx over 10 years for 'Green Energy Corridors' to evacuate Rajasthan/Gujarat solar). Reliance's ₹75,000 Cr new energy push creates optionality play. Oil price correlation: when Brent touches $100/barrel, Reliance O2C petrochemicals margins compress; when $70, they expand. Currency headwind: when rupee weakens, oil import costs rise but refinery margins improve (net neutral).

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: World's largest refining complex at Jamnagar (1.4 million barrels/day). O2C (Oil to Chemicals) business generates ₹6 lakh crore+ revenue. Investing ₹75,000 Cr in new energy (40 GW solar, green hydrogen at Dhirubhai Ambani Green Energy Giga Complex).

holdings.name: ONGC
holdings.description: India's largest oil and gas exploration company producing 70% of India's crude oil and 62% of natural gas. Government holds 58%. Owns 54% of HPCL. ONGC Videsh has overseas assets in 15+ countries. Dividend yield of 5-6%.

holdings.name: NTPC
holdings.description: India's largest power generator — 73 GW operational, 30 GW under construction. Targets 60 GW renewable by 2032 (solar parks, wind, hydro). Government holds 51%. High dividend yield, regulated returns, AAA-rated.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company. Produces 700+ MT annually — monopoly in Indian coal sector with 7 subsidiaries. Government holds 63%. Dividend yield of 6-8%. Essential for India's 200 GW thermal power generation base.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corp
holdings.description: India's monopoly inter-state power transmission utility — 70% of India's grid. 175,000+ circuit km of HV lines, 264 substations. Government holds 51%. Regulated 15.5% post-tax returns. Building Green Energy Corridors for 50 GW solar.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil company by revenue — Fortune 500 ranked company. 11 refineries (82 MMTPA combined capacity), 33,000+ IndianOil fuel stations, Indane LPG (10 crore+ households) and SERVO lubricants. Expanding into petrochemicals and green hydrogen.

holdings.name: BPCL
holdings.description: Bharat Petroleum — integrated oil company. 3 refineries (38 MMTPA), 19,000+ petrol pumps, 6,200 LPG distributors. Government holds 52.98%. Mega Bina refinery expansion underway. Strong petrochemicals growth roadmap.

holdings.name: GAIL India
holdings.description: India's largest natural gas marketing and transmission company. 12,000+ km cross-country pipeline network. City gas distribution via MGL, IGL and others. Beneficiary of India's gas economy push (current 6% of energy mix targeting 15% by 2030).

holdings.name: Adani Green Energy
holdings.description: India's largest renewable energy company with 10+ GW operational solar and wind capacity. Targeting 50 GW by 2030 as part of Adani Green's ambitious clean energy expansion. Secured India's largest single solar power order from SECI.

holdings.name: Torrent Power
holdings.description: Gujarat-based integrated power utility. Generation (4 GW+ gas, solar), transmission and distribution in Ahmedabad, Surat, Gandhinagar and Agra. One of India's most profitable private power distribution licensees. Strong renewable capacity addition track.

name: ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund - ICICI Prudential Nifty Oil & Gas ETF

index.name: Nifty Oil & Gas Index
index.description: ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund - ICICI Prudential Nifty Oil & Gas ETF tracks the Nifty Oil & Gas Index index.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: Jamnagar refinery complex (world's largest single-location) processes 1.4 MBPD crude. Petchem margins stabilising after 3-year pressure. GRM (Gross Refining Margin) recovering as complex refinery premium expands vs simple Asian peers.

holdings.name: Oil & Natural Gas Corporation
holdings.description: India's largest oil and gas explorer. Offshore Bombay High, KG Basin and global OVL subsidiaries. Targeting 2x oil production by 2030 from deep-water and EOR. Subsidiary HPCL integration strengthens downstream positioning.

holdings.name: Indian Oil Corporation
holdings.description: 35,000+ petrol pumps and 11 refineries. India's largest fuel retailer. LNG terminals, city gas distribution, EV charging hubs — diversifying the energy transition portfolio strategically.

holdings.name: Bharat Petroleum Corporation
holdings.description: India's 2nd largest fuel retailer. Mumbai and Kochi refineries. Petrochemical expansion at Bina refinery. IFP Technip license for complex refining. Potential privatisation upside: government divesting 52.98% stake.

holdings.name: GAIL (India)
holdings.description: Natural gas transmission pipeline monopoly — 16,000+ km HVJ Pipeline and DVPL network. India's gas demand growing with city gas, fertilizer and power sector consumption. LNG re-gasification terminal at Dabhol expanding.

holdings.name: Petronet LNG
holdings.description: Dahej LNG terminal (world's 11th largest) handles 80%+ of India's LNG imports. Guaranteed throughput from Shell and ExxonMobil long-term contracts. Regulated tariff plus spot LNG income floor. 3rd terminal under development.

name: Groww Mutual Fund - Groww BSE Power ETF

index.name: BSE Power Index - TRI
index.description: Groww Mutual Fund - Groww BSE Power ETF tracks the BSE Power Index - TRI index.

holdings.name: NTPC Limited
holdings.description: India's largest power company. 73 GW operational (thermal + hydro + solar) targeting 130 GW by 2032. NTPC Green Energy subsidiary (solar + wind parks) separately tracking renewable pivot. Tariff-regulated revenue ensures predictability.

holdings.name: Power Grid Corporation
holdings.description: Backbone of India's electricity grid — 70%+ inter-state transmission. Regulated ROE 15-16% on every ₹100 invested. HVDC Green Energy Corridors being built: 13 new inter-state projects to evacuate renewable power from Rajasthan/Gujarat.

holdings.name: Adani Power
holdings.description: India's largest private power generator (15 GW thermal). Supplies to 7 states under long-term PPAs. Kawai and Godda ultra-super-critical plants among most efficient. Bangladesh Godda plant exports electricity internationally.

holdings.name: Tata Power
holdings.description: Diversified power play: Mumbai Coastal/Trombay thermal, Mundra UMPP, 4 GW operational solar rooftop+utility. EV charging network (Tata Power EV) — 5,000+ chargers. Captive power supply and solar EPC business growing 40%+.

holdings.name: JSW Energy
holdings.description: JSW Group's energy arm. Targeting 20 GW renewable capacity by 2030 (from 7 GW today). Solar parks in Rajasthan, Karnataka and Maharashtra. Pumped hydro storage for round-the-clock renewable supply under planning.

holdings.name: Torrent Power
holdings.description: Ahmedabad and Surat distribution franchise — regulated, zero bad debt. Renewables: wind + solar in Gujarat and Rajasthan. City gas distribution (Ahmedabad, Vadodara) adds gas utility revenue. High-quality management track record.
